Tropical Cyclone Chido (2025): Rapid Report

Lincole Jiang, Majd Abid, Lawrence Chillrud, G. Brooke Anderson, Robbie M. Parks

2026-04-16

Summary

Data status: Provisional — not yet reanalyzed (US-PROVISIONAL)

Tropical Cyclone Chido (2025) was active from 2024-12-05 12:00:00 to 2024-12-17 12:00:00 UTC. Based on IBTrACS data obtained 04/16/2026, 4,134,501 people experienced tropical cyclone-force (>= 17.49 m/s) winds and 692,084.2 people experienced hurricane-force (>= 32.90 m/s) winds across Comoros, Mozambique, Madagascar, Mauritius. No areas experienced Category 5 winds.

Table 1: Population exposed by country and storm intensity
Country Population exposed (tropical cyclone-force) Population exposed (hurricane-force)
Mozambique 3,369,433 691,831
Comoros 385,406 0
Madagascar 379,409 0
Mauritius 253 253

When accounting for total population, the table below shows the percentage of each country’s population exposed.

Table 2: Percentage of total population exposed by country and storm intensity
Country % Population exposed (tropical cyclone-force) % Population exposed (hurricane-force)
Mozambique 10.8 2.2
Comoros 47.9 0.0
Madagascar 1.3 0.0
Mauritius 0.0 0.0

The spatial extent of the impacts is illustrated in Figure 1, which maps exposure by wind intensity threshold at the second administrative unit (ADM2) level along with the storm track of Chido.

Figure 1: Spatial distribution of exposure

Characterizing exposed areas

Figure 2 shows the distribution of relative deprivation (measured by population-weighted GRDI, aggregated to ADM2 level) and population for each exposed area, categorized by exposure status.

Figure 2: Socioeconomic and population distribution of exposed areas
